title: Economical Nanopore LSK Sequencing: Adapted Protocols with Homebrew Reagents
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3ffi0","text":"This protocol introduces a cost-effective alternative for end repair and dA-tailing in DNA library preparation, particularly tailored for PCR samples with an N50 of 3 kb. By employing a homebrew end repair reagent solution, this method replaces the use of the NEBNext® Ultra™ II End Repair/dA-Tailing Module recommended by Nanopore. Optimization of reagent quantities and a bead-free purification method are combined to achieve efficient adapter ligation while minimizing costs. Notably, an extended incubation time during adapter ligation enhances efficiency. This resource provides a strategic approach for researchers aiming to customize their sequencing workflows while achieving optimal results and substantial savings.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"flk74","text":"Optimized Homebrew Approach: This protocol features a unique homebrew end repair reagent solution as a cost-effective alternative to the Nanopore-recommended NEBNext® Ultra™ II End Repair/dA-Tailing Module. Adhere to the specified volumes meticulously to achieve successful results with your customized reagent.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"h17o","text":"Bead-Free Purification: The protocol employs a bead-free PEG/NaCl precipitation method for efficient purification. Ensure precise centrifugation to maintain DNA pellet integrity and maximize recovery rates.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"71dr9","text":"Strategic Ligation Enhancement: An extended incubation period during adapter ligation enhances efficiency. Dedicate adequate time to this step to optimize adapter ligation results.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":31}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"5r3b8","text":"1. Ethanol Flammability: Ethanol is highly flammable. Use caution when handling and storing ethanol solutions. Work in well-ventilated areas away from open flames, sparks, or heat sources.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Add the materials in the following table sequentially. %s %s MaterialsQuantityDNA800 – 1600 ngH2OBring up to a volume of 43 ul2X PNK/Taq Buffer50 ulATP (25 mM)1 uldNTP (10 mM)5 ulTaq polymerase0.5 ul (2.5 U)T4 PNK0.5 ul (5 U) 1. Due to the high concentration of PEG in the solution, it may be slightly viscous. Gently invert several times to ensure thorough and uniform mixing.
Incubate at 37 °C for 00:30:00 1. T4 PNK will add phosphate to the 5' ends of both strands of DNA
Incubate at 65 °C for 00:20:00 T4 PNK will be inactivated, and Taq polymerase will begin repairing DNA ends and adding A-tails.
For the subsequent purification, commercially available spin-column or magnetic bead-based methods can be used. Here, to save costs, we will use the PEG/NaCl precipitation method.
Add 15 µL of NP 1. Due to the high concentration of PEG in the solution, it may be slightly viscous. Gently invert several times to ensure thorough and uniform mixing.
Centrifuge at maximum speed (at least 13000 rpm) for 00:15:00 .
Carefully remove the supernatant using a pipette. Most of the time, the pellet is not visible to the naked eye, so pay attention to the orientation during centrifugation
Add 200 µL of 80% ethanol. Sometimes the pellet becomes more visible after adding ethanol.
Centrifuge at maximum speed (at least 8000 rpm) for 00:02:00
Carefully remove the supernatant using a pipette.
Add 200 µL of 80% ethanol.
Centrifuge at maximum speed (at least 8000 rpm) for 2 minutes.
Carefully remove the supernatant using a pipette.
Wait for approximately 00:10:00 to ensure complete ethanol evaporation. Prevent excessive drying of the DNA pellet. Prolonged periods can result in DNA breakage.
Add 25 µL of elution buffer. Optional: Measure the DNA concentration. Normally, the DNA concentration should be >30 ng/μL.

When using LNB (with ATP) provided by Nanopore %s %s ReagentVolumeDNA400 ng (for samples with N50 of 3 kb)H2OBring up to a volume of 15 μlLigation Buffer (LNB)6.25 μlT4 Ligase2.5 μlLigation Adapter1.25 μl When using homemade 4X Quick Ligase Buffer (without ATP) %s %s ReagentVolume1DNA400 ng (for samples with N50 of 3 kb)2H2OBring up to a volume of 15.25 μl3Ligation Buffer (LNB)6.25 μl4T4 Ligase2.5 μl5Ligation Adapter1 μl Note that the reagent amounts used in this step are about 1/4 of the manufacturer's recommended volume. This means that the ligation sequencing kit, which was originally designed for 6 uses, can now be utilized for 24 uses. This adjustment is primarily due to our utilization of a bead-free purification method later on, which significantly enhances the recovery rate.

Incubate at room temperature for 01:00:00 . Despite the official recommendation of a 5-minute reaction, based on our own experience, a 60-minute reaction significantly improves adapter ligation efficiency.

Add 3 µL of NP 1. Due to the high concentration of PEG in the solution, it may be slightly viscous. Gently invert several times to ensure thorough and uniform mixing. 2. Normally, PEG and NaCl buffer is required for DNA precipitation. However, it's evident that LNB buffer already contains a high concentration of PEG, so adding NP alone is sufficient for DNA precipitation. For more information, refer to: https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7erhjd6 .

Centrifuge at maximum speed (at least 13000 rpm) for at least 00:15:00 (If a higher recovery rate is needed, centrifuging for up to one hour is also possible). To prevent overheating, it is recommended to use a refrigerated centrifuge. If a refrigerated centrifuge is unavailable, it is advised to perform centrifugation in two steps of 10 minutes each, with a 10-minute interval in between.
Carefully remove the supernatant using a pipette. Most of the time, the pellet is not visible to the naked eye, so pay attention to the orientation during centrifugation

Add 200 uL of LFB wash buffer Note that in this step, use the LFB wash buffer instead of alcohol. This is because the DNA now has motor proteins attached, and using alcohol could disrupt the motor proteins.
Carefully remove the supernatant using a pipette.
Repeat the above washing step twice.
Add 20 µL of EB buffer (provided by the Ligation Sequencing Kit). Optional: Measure the DNA concentration. Normally, the DNA concentration should be >15 ng/μL.
Centrifuge at maximum speed (at least 8000 rpm) for 00:02:00
creator: Jie Hao Ou
